title: Compressed Air Leak Cost Calculator
description: Industrial-grade tool to quantify energy and financial losses from compressed air leaks, based on ISO 50001, Lean Six Sigma, and WERC guidelines.

inputs.label: System Pressure
inputs.businessContext: Operating pressure of the compressed air system; higher pressure increases leak flow rate.

inputs.label: Equivalent Leak Diameter
inputs.businessContext: Diameter of a round orifice representing the average leak size. Common sizes: 1/8", 1/4", 1/2".

inputs.label: Number of Leaks
inputs.businessContext: Total number of identified or estimated leaks in the system.

inputs.label: Annual Operating Hours
inputs.businessContext: Total hours the compressor runs per year. Continuous operation = 8760 h.

inputs.label: Electricity Cost
inputs.businessContext: Average blended cost of electricity per kilowatt-hour.

inputs.label: Compressor Efficiency
inputs.businessContext: Overall efficiency of the compressor system (motor + drive + compressor). Typical range 70-85%.

inputs.label: Leak Type
inputs.businessContext: Geometry of the leak affects discharge coefficient. Round orifice is most common.

inputs.label: Include Maintenance Cost
inputs.businessContext: If true, adds estimated labor and material cost for leak repair.

rules.message: System pressure must be between 60 and 200 psig.
Leak diameter must be between 0.01 and 1.0 inches.
Compressor efficiency must be between 50% and 95%.

thresholds.message: Annual leak cost exceeds $50,000. Immediate action recommended.
Compressor efficiency below 70%. Consider maintenance or upgrade.
Leak diameter > 0.25 inches indicates large leaks. Prioritize repair.

formulas.label: Leak Flow Rate per Leak
formulas.description: Calculates the volumetric flow rate of air through a single leak using orifice equation (ISO 6358).

variables.C_d: discharge coefficient (0.65 for round orifice, 0.55 for crack, 0.75 for threaded fitting)
variables.A: cross-sectional area of leak (sq ft) = pi * (leak_diameter/2)^2 / 144

formulas.label: Total Leak Flow
formulas.description: Total volumetric flow rate from all leaks.

formulas.label: Power Consumption due to Leaks
formulas.description: Power required to compress the leaked air, accounting for compressor efficiency.

formulas.label: Annual Energy Consumption
formulas.description: Total energy consumed by leaks over a year.

formulas.label: Annual Maintenance Cost
formulas.description: Estimated cost to repair leaks (labor + materials). Only included if include_maintenance_cost is true.

formulas.label: Total Annual Leak Cost
formulas.description: Primary result: total financial impact of leaks.

primary.label: Total Annual Leak Cost
primary.description: Primary financial metric combining energy and maintenance costs.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Pressure Drop Effect
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Leaks cause system pressure drop, reducing end-use efficiency and increasing compressor load.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Unplanned Maintenance
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Frequent cycling and overloading due to leaks increase wear on compressor components.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Production Downtime
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Insufficient pressure due to leaks can cause production line stoppages.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Carbon Emissions
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Excess energy consumption from leaks increases CO2 footprint (approx. 0.9 lb CO2 per kWh).

suggestedActions.label: Conduct Ultrasonic Leak Survey
suggestedActions.description: Use ultrasonic detectors to locate and quantify leaks (ISO 50001 best practice).

suggestedActions.label: Prioritize Repairs by Leak Size
suggestedActions.description: Fix leaks > 1/4 inch first, then medium leaks. Use Six Sigma DMAIC approach.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce System Pressure
suggestedActions.description: Lowering pressure by 2 psi can reduce leak flow by ~1% (Lean energy principle).

suggestedActions.label: Implement Leak Tagging Program
suggestedActions.description: Tag and track leaks with a color-coded system (WERC recommended).

suggestedActions.label: Optimize Compressor Controls
suggestedActions.description: Install variable speed drives or sequencing controls to match demand.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Confidence-Adjusted Annual Cost
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Total annual cost adjusted by a confidence factor based on input variability.
